mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index signals deteriorating real earnings and margin compression, prompting investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day forward returns is not negative and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a tight labor market that intensifies wage-price spiral risks and triggers aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when UNRATE is below 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of elevated consumer prices and a near-zero federal funds rate signals a central bank that is behind the inflation curve, triggering expectations of abrupt future tightening and equity risk-premium expansion that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 280 and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time or the interaction term contributes no marginal explanatory power beyond main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals collapsing rate-sensitive demand and forward earnings risk for the construction and financial sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During monetary tightening cycles, months with negative HOUST changes are associated with positive S&P 500 60-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals acute supply-chain disruption and negative earnings surprises for manufacturing firms, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month INDPRO declines do not coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nominal retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that supports corporate top-line revenue growth and lifts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and 20-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 400 thousand per month signal a deepening labor market recovery and accelerating household income growth that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with PAYEMS increases above 400k are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP validates the aggregate earnings outlook and reduces recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns more than 50% of the time.
